bundle_description: Deep dive into OpenBB's charting pipeline — from raw financial data fetching through transformation, indicator computation, and final Plotly rendering via PyWry.

features.name: Chart Rendering Pipeline
features.description: End-to-end pipeline that transforms fetched financial data into interactive Plotly charts rendered through PyWry or browser backends.

features.name: PlotlyTA Indicator Overlay
features.description: Technical analysis indicator computation and overlay system using PlotlyTA to add moving averages, RSI, MACD, and custom indicators to charts.

features.name: Chart Style & Theming
features.description: Dynamic theming engine that applies consistent color palettes, fonts, and layout configurations across all chart types.

features.name: Data Normalization Layer
features.description: Standardizes heterogeneous data from multiple providers (yFinance, Polygon, Alpha Vantage) into a unified OBBject format for charting.

features.name: Chart Export System
features.description: Multi-format export supporting PNG, SVG, HTML, and interactive standalone files with embedded data.

call_chain.role: Primary entry point that receives an OBBject result, determines chart type, and orchestrates the full rendering pipeline.
Constructs the Plotly subplot grid layout based on the number of indicators and secondary y-axes required.
Routes the OBBject command path (e.g., 'equity.price.historical') to the correct charting function via a registry lookup.
Specific charting function for OHLCV candlestick data — builds traces, adds volume bars, and configures range slider.
Applies final layout configuration including margins, legend position, axis formatting, and responsive breakpoints.
Dispatches the completed figure to the selected rendering backend (PyWry desktop window or browser tab).

chart_rendering_pipeline.explanation: The chart rendering pipeline begins when user code calls to_chart() on an OBBject result. This method inspects the command path that produced the data (e.g., 'equity.price.historical') and uses ChartingRouter to look up the appropriate charting function. The router maintains a registry mapping command paths to specialized chart builders. For price data, price_historical() constructs candlestick traces with volume bars. The figure is built on OpenBBFigure (a Plotly Figure subclass) which adds create_subplots() for multi-panel layouts. After traces are added, _apply_layout() configures axis labels, margins, and theme colors. Finally, Backend.send_figure() dispatches the rendered chart to either a PyWry native window (default desktop experience) or a browser tab.

call_chain.role: Main entry point that accepts a DataFrame and indicator configuration, iterates through requested indicators, and adds each as a trace.
Resolves an indicator name (e.g., 'sma', 'rsi', 'macd') to the corresponding computation and trace-building method.
Performs the actual pandas-based computation for a given technical indicator using configurable window sizes and parameters.
Builds Plotly trace objects for moving average overlays (SMA, EMA, WMA) with configurable line styles and colors.
Places computed indicator traces onto the correct subplot row, handling secondary y-axes for oscillators like RSI.

plotly_ta_indicators.explanation: PlotlyTA is the technical analysis overlay engine. When plot() is called with a DataFrame and a list of desired indicators, it iterates through each one. For each indicator, _get_indicator() resolves the name to a handler — e.g., 'sma' maps to overlap_studies.plot_ma(). The handler first calls calculate_indicator() which uses pandas operations (or pandas-ta if available) to compute the values (e.g., a 20-period simple moving average). The computed Series is then transformed into a Plotly Scatter trace with appropriate styling. Finally, _add_to_subplot() places the trace on the correct subplot — overlays like moving averages go on the main price panel (row 1), while oscillators like RSI get their own dedicated subplot row.

call_chain.role: Applies a named style preset (e.g., 'dark', 'light', 'bloomberg') to the Plotly figure's layout and trace defaults.
Loads a theme definition from a JSON file in the styles/ directory, parsing color palettes, font stacks, and grid settings.
Maps theme color tokens (background, gridline, text, up/down candle colors) to Plotly layout properties.
Sets font family, sizes, and weights for title, axis labels, legend, and hover text from the theme definition.

chart_style_theming.explanation: The theming system uses a preset-based approach. ChartStyle.apply() is called with a style name (defaulting to the OPENBB_CHART_STYLE env var or 'dark'). It calls _load_theme() which reads a JSON definition file from the styles/ directory. This JSON contains color palettes (background, gridlines, up/down candle colors, trace color cycle), font configurations, and layout tweaks. The apply method then delegates to _apply_colors() and _apply_fonts() which translate these tokens into Plotly's template system, modifying layout.template.layout for global defaults and layout.template.data for trace-level defaults like scatter line colors and bar fill colors.
